ticket.title: Add multiply(a, b) to the toy calculator
acceptance_criteria: Function signature is `multiply(a: int, b: int) -> int` and returns the integer product of the two arguments.
Edge cases covered: multiply(0, n) and multiply(n, 0) return 0; multiply(-2, 3) returns -6.
A pytest test `test_multiply_returns_product` lives in `tests/test_calculator.py` and exercises both the happy path and the negative-input edge case.
